items.title: Cathie Wood Increases Bet On Nvidia And AMD Despite China Chip Hurdles, Ark Dumps Microsoft Stock Worth $9.7 Million
items.description: Ark Invest made major portfolio shifts on Tuesday April 22, 2025, boosting stakes in Nvidia, AMD, and Airbnb while trimming Microsoft and others to reposition amid U.S. chip export restrictions and shifting tech trends.

items.title: Cloud Infrastructure Growth Could Cool This Year Despite AI Excitement, Analyst Says
items.description: Amazon stock has been hit by fears about tariffs and e-commerce but analysts are warning about cloud risks as well.

items.title: CoreWeave Stock Gets a Wave of Buy Ratings. But No One Is Denying the Risk.
items.description: CoreWeave stock has been on a wild ride since its March IPO. Analysts are bullish from here but acknowledge plenty of risk in CoreWeave’s rapid growth.

items.title: Mizuho Is Bullish on Microsoft’s (MSFT) Cloud Businesses
items.description: Microsoft’s (MSFT) cloud-infrastructure unit, Azure, is well-positioned to deliver “impressive..growth” over the long term, Gregg Moskowitz, an analyst at Japanese bank Mizuho, wrote recently. Moskowitz has a $475 price target and an Outperform rating on MSFT. items.title: Where Will Palantir Stock Be in 3 Years?
items.description: With shares up 647% since 2022, Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) has performed spectacularly over the last three years, highlighting the potentially life-changing power of tech investing.  Since its founding in 2003, Palantir has emerged as a leader in big data analytics, enabling organizations to uncover actionable insights, detect fraud, and optimize their operations by sifting through vast volumes of information.  Over the last three years, Palantir's shares have surged alongside other tech stocks, such as Microsoft and Nvidia, which also have exposure to the AI industry.

items.title: Why Microsoft Stock Was Getting Slammed Today
items.description: The kickoff of a high-profile antitrust case against a peer and an analyst's price target reduction put the hurt on Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) shares Monday.  The U.S. Department of Justice's artificial intelligence (AI)-related suit against the company began.  Alphabet, whose core asset is its familiar search engine Google, has landed in hot legal water before, but the DOJ seems particularly determined this time to cut the company down to size.
